pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 40 - creates input with json={facility_main_power:2500}
setup_task.sh line 40 - creates input with node=main_meter
export_result.sh line 28 - queries for feed name='current_carbon_intensity'; verifier.py line 31 checks for this feed
export_result.sh line 48 - queries for dashboard name='Sustainability_Display'; verifier.py line 73 checks for this name

Web search (IEA Global Energy & CO2 Status Report 2019) confirms 0.475 kg CO2/kWh as a historically used average carbon intensity for electricity. Verified in verifier.py line 54 checks for '2:0.475' in process chain
Standard conversion factor from Watts to Kilowatts. Verified in verifier.py line 43 checks for '2:0.001' in process chain; task.json description line 5 specifies 'multiply by 0.001'

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a carbon footprint monitoring pipeline in Emoncms. All metadata claims are verified: the input 'facility_main_power' is created with node 'main_meter' and generates synthetic power data in the range 2000-5000W. The task requires applying a scale factor of 0.001 (W to kW conversion) and carbon factor of 0.475 kg CO2/kWh to create a feed named 'current_carbon_intensity'. The carbon factor of 0.475 kg CO2/kWh is a historically used global average from the IEA (as of 2019) and represents typical electricity carbon intensity. A dashboard named 'Sustainability_Display' must be created with a Dial or Gauge widget linked to the feed. All data is synthetically generated within the task setup scripts.
